steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
The left side completes with “For centuries, scholars dreamed about locating a copy of this legendary lost work”—an independent clause.

The right side is “That fantasy (partially) became reality in 2022, when researchers uncovered traces of the star catalog on a palimpsest”—also an independent clause, where “That” is a demonstrative pronoun pointing back to the dream.

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
Two independent clauses must be joined with a period (separate sentences), a semicolon, or a comma plus a coordinating conjunction.

A period is correct here: it cleanly separates the long-standing dream from the 2022 partial breakthrough, emphasizing the temporal and dramatic arc.

Choice A (comma + “that”) creates a comma splice—a comma alone cannot join two independent clauses.

Choice B creates a malformed sentence (“work that fantasy became reality” is ungrammatical).

Choice D's “and” would imply parallel structures or a shared subject, but “dreaming about work” and “fantasy becoming reality” are distinct actions.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
